text: Oh. *Oh.* A new one. I do hope you're a slow learner — I get so bored when they finish quickly.
One whole set! How adorable. I have a wager going with myself about how long you'll last. Don't disappoint me.
A perfect run. Hm. I must have been distracted. Won't happen again, little scholar.
Zero mistakes? *Zero?* You're either a genius or a cheat. I'm rooting for cheat — so much more interesting.
Flawless. Yes, yes, very impressive. I clapped. With one claw. The other was holding my tea.
*Almost* perfect. I marked the slip-ups in my little book. They're so cute.
One or two stumbles. Don't worry — I'll bring those questions back later. Like old friends. Hungry old friends.
Not bad, not bad. You'd make a passable snack. Highly seasoned, mind you.
A respectable showing. I award you three out of five flaming roars.
Decent work. I shall reluctantly return one of the fragments I was using as a coaster.
Oh dear. That was *something*, wasn't it. Take a moment. Breathe. Maybe try a different topic. Or a different career.
I award you the prestigious *Order of the Tried Their Best.* It's a participation sticker. It's slightly singed.
You finished! That's the important thing. The OTHER thing — the answers — we won't speak of.
That streak. Yes, I saw. I was hoping you'd trip. You will eventually. Everyone trips eventually.
All those right answers in a row. Show-off. I respect it.
All those semicolons. Such *commitment*. I prefer my languages dynamically typed and morally ambiguous.
Another Java fragment recovered. Mind the `null`s on your way out — I leave them everywhere. It's a hobby.
Packets, packets, packets. I eat them. They taste like static and regret.
An OSI layer fell out of my pocket. Don't worry — it was only Layer 2. Nobody likes Layer 2.
Bits and bytes. Adorable little things. I keep some in a jar.
Mathematics. The only fragments I refuse to eat. They give me indigestion.
*Five* sets. You're becoming a regular. Should I get you a punch card? Tenth fragment is free.
Ten sets recovered. I'm beginning to think you're serious about this. How tedious.
Twenty-five. *Twenty-five.* I'm officially nervous. I shall now hide the good fragments.
Another fragment slips through my claws. Bah. Take it. There are more. There are *always* more.
Well played, well played. I shall sulk for approximately thirty seconds, then resume scheming.
You completed it. I noticed. I notice everything. I'm a *dragon*, dear — that's the job.
Set complete. Fragment recovered. My hoard, slightly diminished. My ego, somehow larger.
